game_name: Populous II: Trials of the Olympian Gods

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Players learn resource constraints and power costs gradually
Basic divine intervention before advanced effects
Advanced tactical tools, fungus follows Conway's Game of Life
Six legendary Greek mythology heroes enable specialized tactical options
Indestructible neutral threats that force map completion urgency
Random element added to prevent unrecoverable losing positions

typical_tension_triggers: Monster spawning at 75% map control threshold
Inability to recover from disadvantageous position (mitigated by temple system)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cyclical tension tied to manna economy and territorial control; monsters introduce time pressure at late-game threshold; performance-based progression allows dynamic difficulty via map skipping

difficulty_curve.early_game: Low pressure; focus on learning manna system, basic earth/water powers, population management, and terrain shaping
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Moderate pressure; introduction of fire, plants, and people category powers; hero transformations; enemy temples add complexity; strategic power selection becomes critical
difficulty_curve.late_game: High pressure; late-game maps demand sophisticated power chaining; monster spawning forces completion urgency; final boss is Zeus after defeating all Greek deities
notable_spikes: Monster spawn threshold at ~75% map control (indestructible, indiscriminate threats)
Progression to each successive Greek deity opponent (scaling difficulty)

notable_level_patterns.description: When either side controls ~75% of map, ancient Greek monsters (Colossus, Medusa) spawn and wander indiscriminately. Indestructible and create urgency to complete objective before collateral devastation.
Exceptional performance allows skipping multiple maps; poor performance limits advancement to 3 or fewer maps. Creates dynamic difficulty curve within 1000-map campaign.
Awarded 1-5 XP per map based on victory scale, power usage efficiency, and battle length. Allows persistent upgrade of manna costs and power effectiveness across 6 categories.
Player progresses through campaign as demigod facing one Greek deity per map sequence, culminating in Zeus battle for Pantheon entry. Mythological narrative framework.
